title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL startup keeps pool state after auto-create failure
summary: Oracle MySQL startup left a connection pool reference in memory when auto-create schema setup failed.
observed: A failed auto_create path could leave the sink with a non-null pool even though startup did not complete successfully.
expected: Startup failure during auto-create must clear the pool reference.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for auto-create failure pool cleanup.
impact: A partially started sink can make follow-up lifecycle behavior ambiguous.
delivery_semantics: The fix preserves fail-closed startup behavior before messages are acknowledged.
security: Clearing partial runtime state avoids operating with an incompletely validated destination.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL startup clears the pool reference when auto_create fails.
Successful startup still preserves the pool reference.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and Oracle MySQL sink startup tests.
documentation: No separate user documentation is required beyond release-note mention of the lifecycle fix.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the lifecycle fix is published.
